title: GLASS SKIN
subtitle: Christian Ice Co. · 5:34 AM · the second-to-last week
epigraph_upright: Hope as a quiet luminous thing. The star you see only when you stop looking for it. The Star upright is the unforced clarity that arrives in the morning after a long bad night and asks nothing of you in exchange.
epigraph_reversed: Hope that has begun to fog. The glass that won't stay clear long enough to read. The ice plant on the second-to-last Wednesday before it closes, and the morning customer who has come every Wednesday for forty years and does not, yet, know.
scene_description: 5:34 AM at Christian Ice Co., the old block-ice plant on Calliope. The compressors have been running since 4. Delphine — third generation; her grandfather opened the place in 1949 — is on the morning open. The block freezer's glass is fogged the way it always is at this hour; you wipe it once, it fogs again. The neon ICE letters on the parapet outside are on; through the storefront the sky is starting. The plant closes in eleven days. The neighborhood is going to find out from a notice in next week's Picayune; Delphine has not, yet, made the call to Mr Couvillon who has bought a block here every Wednesday since the year she was born.
direction_hint: Run the morning routine. Pull two blocks. Bag two bags. Check the compressor. Open the dock door at 6. Receive the first customer at 6:14 — Mr Couvillon, every Wednesday. Don't wipe the storefront glass. Let it clear on its own.
opening_log_lines: 5:34 AM. Compressors running. The block freezer is the colour the block freezer is.
Through the storefront: the sky is about to start.
Click [b]PHASE: ACTION[/b] anytime for the rules. [b]MOVE ↪[/b] to cross the floor.

inertia_thresholds.label: the sky is doing its blue
a car door slams on Calliope
Mr Couvillon's truck is in the lot
the kid is at the storefront glass
your hands are cold the way your hands are at 6 AM

thresholds.label: DOCK DOOR
thresholds.flavor: Roll-up. Out to the alley and the city's morning.

thresholds.label: STOREFRONT
thresholds.flavor: Glass door onto Calliope. The neon ICE letters above your head.

notes: Star scenario, easy difficulty. Win = open doors at 6 (DOCK DOOR + STOREFRONT), receive Mr Couvillon's morning block, connect 2 visitors. Loss: fogging pin at 12, OR the doors closed at 6:15 (the plant didn't open today, the news travels).
